applicability_notes: Elementwise binary equality comparison. Supports tensor-tensor and tensor-scalar comparison (see call sites: ttnn.eq(input_ids, pad_token_id), ttnn.eq(attention_mask, 0)). Used in models to build padding masks from input_ids or HF-convention attention masks (1=keep, 0=pad). Output is boolean-like (1/0) in same dtype family. Tests exercise uint16/uint32 exact-match comparison after fill/add/sub/square ops.

placement_observations.size_descriptor: small test tensors (1D vectors of 6-8 elements, [1,2] shapes)
placement_observations.source: unit test: ttnn.from_torch(..., layout=ttnn.TILE_LAYOUT, memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG); comparison_result = ttnn.eq(output_tensor, torch_output_tensor)

placement_observations.size_descriptor: same shape as input_a (elementwise comparison reference tensor)
placement_observations.source: unit test: torch_output converted via ttnn.from_torch with TILE_LAYOUT, DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG before ttnn.eq
model call site: pad_mask = ttnn.eq(input_ids, self.pad_token_id) and pad_mask = ttnn.eq(attention_mask, 0); inputs inherit default interleaved placement, no explicit memory_config passed
